summary: Opinion piece challenging the convention of nailing metal shoes onto horses' feet, arguing for natural barefoot hoof function and questioning historical farriery practices.
Cavallo president Carole Herder shares her personal story of transitioning her cutting-horse mare Dot from metal shoes to barefoot with Cavallo hoof boots after a navicular diagnosis.
Case study of Frodo, a laminitic Gypsy Cob whose owner Sue Grice used Cavallo BFB (Big Foot) hoof boots and pads to keep him moving and ease pain.
Explainer on keeping a horse barefoot, covering why some horses thrive without shoes and the factors behind a successful barefoot program, including the roles of the frog, bars and sole.
Guidance on winter hoof care and transitioning a horse to barefoot in the off-season to restore hoof mechanism and prevent conditions like navicular, laminitis and thrush.
History of metal horseshoeing from war-horse origins and a critique of why owners still nail metal to hooves, advocating barefoot hoof care per Carole Herder.
Short customer testimonial from Adam Kittel about a barefoot-advocate farrier and using Cavallo Trek hoof boots on a retired reining mare prone to abscess.
Overview of common hoof problems like seedy toe, thrush and hoof cracks, covering their causes and basic considerations for nutrition, balance and treatment.
Six considerations for building a horse's barefoot program, including choosing a trimmer, hoof mechanism, and encouraging movement.
Q&A with Carole Herder on White Line Disease, explaining what it is, hoof anatomy, and how to prevent or treat it for healthy hooves.
Opinion piece on the welfare of NYC carriage horses, arguing their calm demeanor reflects 'shut down' and that metal shoes on hard pavement harm their hooves and bodies.
Announcement of the new Cavallo Comfort Sleeve, a cotton pastern-protection accessory for use with any Cavallo hoof boot, with a video demo and break-in tips.
